basic_info.title: Final Fantasy XII: The Zodiac Age
basic_info.review: Final Fantasy XII is a landmark JRPG featuring an expansive world, innovative Gambit system, and mature storytelling that redefined the series' direction.

alternative_names: ファイナルファンタジーXII (Japanese)
Final Fantasy XII International: The Zodiac Job System

play_today.platform: Nintendo Switch / Xbox One / PS4 (Zodiac Age)
play_today.details: The definitive version with Job System, faster gameplay speed, quality-of-life improvements including skip cutscenes and auto-battle. Best experience on modern platforms.

description.synopsis: Vaan, a young thief from Dalmasca, becomes entangled in a war between nations while uncovering a conspiracy involving the Empire and his friend Basch.
key_features: Innovative Gambit system for automated combat strategies
Deep Job System (Zodiac Age version) with 12 license boards
Complex political storyline about war and betrayal
description.long_description: Final Fantasy XII, released for PlayStation 2 in March 2006 (Japan) and October 2006 (North America), is a landmark JRPG developed by Square Enix. Directed by Hiroyuki Ito and Akihiko Yoshida, it introduced the Gambit system allowing players to create automated battle strategies based on conditions (e.g., 'IF ally HP < 70% THEN heal'). The Zodiac Age version (2017) added the Job System with 12 different license boards for character customization, faster gameplay speed (2x original), and quality-of-life improvements including skip cutscenes and auto-battle. With over 60 hours of content and an iconic soundtrack by Hitoshi Sakimoto, it's widely considered one of the greatest JRPGs ever made.
legacy_and_impact: Introduced Gambit system influencing later RPGs
Set new standards for open-world exploration in JRPGs
Zodiac Age version became a model for remakes
Influenced Final Fantasy XIV's development direction
Continues to receive community mods and appreciation decades after release
